title: Coronaria coeli-rosa
intro: Coronaria coeli-rosa, also known as Corn Poppy or Corn Rose, is a flowering plant in the poppy family (Papaveraceae) that is native to Europe and Western Asia. The health benefits of Coronaria coeli-rosa include its antispasmodic, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties, which can help to relieve menstrual cramps, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing. The bioactive constituents of this plant include alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, such as protopine and sanguinarine, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Coronaria coeli-rosa is commonly used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health issues, including digestive problems, fever, and skin conditions.
taxonomy: Coronaria coeli-rosa is classified under the family Boraginaceae. It is a species of flowering plant, native to Europe and parts of Asia. The genus Coronaria belongs to the order Boraginales. Coronaria coeli-rosa is a member of the subfamily Boraginoideae. Coronaria coeli-rosa is further classified into the subgenus Rytidolobus.
uses: Coronaria coeli-rosa, also known as Glory of the Snow, has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. Its roots and leaves are said to have been used to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and digestive issues. In addition, Coronaria coeli-rosa has been used to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing. However, further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using this plant for medicinal purposes. Traditional use of Coronaria coeli-rosa should be approached with caution and under the guidance of a qualified healthcare professional.
